items.text: crates/liminal/src/protocol/mod.rs exists with pub mod declarations for all protocol submodules and no logic (CN3).
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs defines ProtocolError with variants for every error condition and numeric reason codes.
Frame header is a fixed-size binary prefix containing frame type (u8), flags (u8), stream ID (u32), and payload length (u32).

FrameType enum covers all frame categories: Connect, ConnectAck, ConnectError, Disconnect, Subscribe, SubscribeAck, SubscribeError, Unsubscribe, Publish, PublishAck, PublishError, ConversationOpen, ConversationMessage, ConversationClose, ConversationError, Accept, Defer, Reject, Ping, Pong.
Each Frame variant holds typed fields matching its frame category, not raw bytes.
All Frame variants and FrameType implement Debug (CN4).
Frame construction validates invariants: stream 0 for control frames, stream >= 1 for application frames (CN7).
encode() writes a frame to a byte buffer as header + payload with correct length prefix.
decode() reads a frame from a byte buffer, returning the frame and the number of bytes consumed.
decode() returns ProtocolError for malformed input, never panics (CN6).
Round-trip property holds: decode(encode(frame)) == frame for every frame type.
decode() skips unknown frame types by reading the length prefix and discarding the payload (CN9).
MessageEnvelope contains schema_id (content type hash), causal_context, and payload bytes.
MessageEnvelope serializes deterministically: the same fields always produce the same byte sequence.
Publish and ConversationMessage frames carry a MessageEnvelope, not raw payload bytes.
CausalContext type represents an optional parent message reference and a vector clock entry.
CausalContext is extractable from a message-carrying frame without deserializing the application payload (P1).
CausalContext serializes as part of the MessageEnvelope, not as a separate frame field.
ConnectionState enum defines states: Connecting, Authenticating, Active, Closing, Closed.
State machine rejects frames invalid for the current state and returns ProtocolError with the appropriate reason code.
Connect frame carries the client's supported protocol version range and an opaque auth token.
ConnectAck frame carries the selected protocol version and server capabilities.
Ping/Pong frames are valid only in the Active state and travel on stream 0.
ProtocolVersion type defines major and minor version components.
Version negotiation selects the highest mutually supported version from the client's range and the server's supported set.
Incompatible version ranges produce ConnectError with a version-mismatch reason code.
StreamId is a u32 carried in the frame header, with 0 reserved for connection control (CN7).
StreamTable tracks active streams per connection with their state (subscribing, active, closing).
Client-initiated streams use odd IDs; server-initiated streams (incoming conversation offers) use even IDs.
Accept, Defer, and Reject are distinct frame types carrying the referenced message ID on the same stream as the original publish (CN8).
Per-stream pressure state tracks outstanding (unacknowledged) messages and transitions between normal, deferred, and rejecting.
Subscribe frame includes max_in_flight declaration so the bus knows consumer capacity.
Subscribe frame includes an array of accepted schema IDs (content type hashes) the subscriber can handle.
SubscribeAck frame includes the selected schema ID the bus will use for messages on that subscription.
Schema mismatch (no overlap between subscriber's accepted schemas and channel's declared schema) produces SubscribeError with a schema-incompatible reason code.
